title: Author client hook config templates (Claude Code settings.json + Codex hooks.json)
description: Author the two static client-config templates that fabric init merges into the user's project: (1) packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/claude-code.json — fragment template for hooks.Stop[] entry registering archive-hint.cjs; (2) packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json — full hooks file (NOT toml) for Codex CLI Stop event registering archive-hint.cjs. Both reference the script via project-relative path .claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs (Claude) or .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs (Codex). Cursor explicitly excluded — documented in planning-context.md as schema deviation.

files.target: Claude Code Stop-hook fragment for merge-insertion
files.change: JSON fragment matching schema verified in doctor.test.ts:141 — `{"hooks":{"Stop":[{"matcher":"*","hooks":[{"type":"command","command":".claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs"}]}]}}`. Top-level `hooks` key, per-event `Stop` array, entry with matcher='*' (match all), hooks sub-array with single {type:'command', command:'<project-relative path>'}. Template is a partial — TASK-005 init wiring deepMerges this into existing .claude/settings.json without clobbering mcpServers/permissions/other hooks.

files.target: Codex CLI hooks.json full file template
files.change: Full Codex hooks.json file shape — {events:{Stop:[{command:'.codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs'}]}} (or whichever exact shape Codex 0.x uses; lock at task execution time by reading developers.openai.com/codex/hooks IF reachable, else fall back to the {events:{Stop:[{command}]}} pattern matching the existing templates/codex-hooks/fabric-stop-reminder.cjs reference comments). Note path uses .codex/hooks/ subdir — TASK-005 init wiring copies archive-hint.cjs into both .claude/hooks/ AND .codex/hooks/ destinations.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts:141-162 to confirm Claude settings.json hooks.Stop[] schema verbatim
Read packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts:157 to confirm Codex hooks file path is .codex/hooks.json (NOT .toml)

Read existing templates/codex-hooks/fabric-stop-reminder.cjs to infer Codex hooks.json registration shape from script comments OR fetch developers.openai.com/codex/hooks if WebFetch available at execution time
Author claude-code.json fragment matching doctor.test.ts:141 schema exactly

Author codex-hooks.json with {events:{Stop:[{command:'.codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs'}]}} shape; if execution-time research reveals different shape, correct on the fly (this template is a v2 starter — easy to iterate)
Add file-level comment header in each (JSON5-style if needed; OR README.md sibling) explaining 'this is a fragment merged by fabric init, not a standalone file'
Commit: `feat(cli): add hook config templates for Claude Code and Codex CLI`

reference.pattern: Static JSON template fragments for client config merge
reference.examples: Claude schema is fully pinned by doctor.test.ts:141 fixture — copy it. Codex schema is partially documented; rely on resolver.ts:157 for file location and existing templates/codex-hooks/ for registration shape; if any ambiguity surfaces at execution, lock via execution-time WebFetch and document in commit message.

criteria: claude-code.json structure matches doctor.test.ts:141 schema verbatim — verifiable by deep-equal in TASK-006 test
claude-code.json command path is `.claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs` (project-relative, matches TASK-005 install destination)
Both files include a header comment explaining merge-fragment semantics OR sibling README.md documents it
No Cursor config file exists in templates/hooks/configs/ (Cursor explicitly out of scope, documented in planning-context.md)

rationale.chosen_approach: Two static JSON template files in templates/hooks/configs/ — Claude Code fragment for merge, Codex full file for write-or-merge
alternatives_considered: Generate configs programmatically in init.ts — rejected: harder to review, harder for users to copy-paste manually for non-init flows
Single combined config — rejected: shapes differ between clients (Claude top-level hooks key, Codex top-level events key)
Include Cursor template — rejected: no documented Cursor Stop-hook surface; planning-context.md documents this as schema deviation
decision_factors: resolver.ts:157 confirms Codex file is JSON not TOML
Static templates are reviewable in PR diff as plain JSON
rationale.tradeoffs: Codex schema may evolve (Codex CLI v1.x is moving target) — accepted: rc.2 ships with documented assumption + easy follow-up patch

manual_checks: Open both JSON files; confirm they parse via `node -e 'JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(...))'`
Diff claude-code.json against doctor.test.ts:141 fixture verbatim
Verify command paths use forward-slashes (cross-platform safe in JSON config; tools normalize at runtime)
success_metrics: Both configs parse as valid JSON
TASK-006 contract test deep-equals templates against fixtures
